question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user use the spender.id gte filter to enumerate all NFTs approved to spenders with IDs greater than a known value, systematically walking through the spender ID space to discover all accounts that have been granted transfer authority over a victim account's NFTs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user use the spender.id lte filter to enumerate all NFTs approved to spenders with IDs less than or equal to a known value, systematically walking through the spender ID space in reverse to discover all accounts that have been granted transfer authority over a victim account's NFTs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ownerAccountId is fixed at $1 in the SQL query but the spenderIdFilters are appended to the params array dynamically, to craft a filter combination where the spender condition references the wrong parameter index and returns NFT approvals for a different owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftViewModel exposing the spender field (which is the approved spender's account ID) to confirm whether a specific account has been granted NFT transfer approval, using the API as a binary oracle for authorization state without being the owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the query.params.unshift(accountId, query.limit) pattern in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() — where accountId is inserted at position $1 and limit at $2 — to cause a parameter index shift if the conditions array already contains positional references, returning staking rewards for a different account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityService.getEncodedId() function's handling of the evmAddress field — where a non-null evmAddress triggers a database lookup via getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() — to use the API as an EVM-address-to-account-ID mapping oracle, enabling targeted enumeration of NFT approvals for accounts identified only by their EVM address?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityService.entityFromEvmAddressQuery not filtering by deleted status (WHERE deleted <> true is present but coalesce is not used as in entityFromAliasQuery) to resolve EVM addresses of deleted accounts and enumerate their historical NFT spender approvals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityService.entityFromAliasQuery using coalesce(deleted, false) <> true (which treats NULL deleted as false/not-deleted) to resolve aliases of accounts with NULL deleted status and enumerate their NFT spender approvals, potentially accessing accounts in an ambiguous deletion state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id eq filter combined with a spender.id eq filter to precisely target a specific token's NFT approvals for a specific spender, confirming whether a particular authorization grant exists without being the owner or the spender?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftService.getSubQuery() constructing the IN clause for spenderIdInFilters as `${Nft.SPENDER} in (${paramsForCondition})` to supply a large number of spender.id eq filters, causing the IN clause to grow unboundedly and potentially bypass query plan optimizations that would otherwise limit result exposure?
